{{Shinto Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:Yoshitsune Shrine, Biratori, 2024.jpg|200px|{{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] |所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Honchō 119-1 |緯度度 = 42 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 32.3 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 142 |経度分 = 8 |経度秒 = 10.4 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |例祭 = |本殿 = }} '''Yoshitsune Shrine''' ({{ill|義経神社|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Honchō 119-1. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Until {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was formally known as "Yoshitsune-sha." A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * In {{ill|1876|ja|1876年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira Mountain|ja|ハヨピラ山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book |title=日高国志料 沙流郡之部 [Hidaka Country Records, Sarugu District Section] |author=御子柴五百彦 |year=1892 |page=18}}). * In {{ill|1884|ja|1884th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 17th year of Meiji) in August – {{ill|Prince Komatsu Akihito|ja|小松宮彰仁親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited and granted offerings for worship. * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the {{ill|Sarugu River|ja|沙流川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and landslides. * In {{ill|1901|ja|1901st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * On November 13, {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the {{ill|Crown Prince|ja|皇太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from {{ill|Kanroji|ja|甘露寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, {{ill|1942|ja|1942nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * On June 19, {{ill|1946|ja|1946年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, {{ill|1961|ja|1961st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in {{ill|1791|ja|1791st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in {{ill|1798|ja|1798th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited {{ill|Ezochi|ja|蝦夷地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and observed that the {{ill|Ainu|ja|アイヌ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; and that in either {{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of Kansei) or {{ill|1802|ja|1802nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|ja|享和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoshitsune Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] [[Category:Kondō Shigekurō]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * In {{ill|1876|ja|1876年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira Mountain|ja|ハヨピラ山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book |title=日高国志料 沙流郡之部 [Hidaka Country Records, Sarugu District Section] |author=御子柴五百彦 |year=1892 |page=18}}). * In {{ill|1884|ja|1884th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 17th year of Meiji) in August – {{ill|Prince Komatsu Akihito|ja|小松宮彰仁親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited and granted offerings for worship. * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the {{ill|Sarugu River|ja|沙流川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and landslides. * In {{ill|1901|ja|1901st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * On November 13, {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the {{ill|Crown Prince|ja|皇太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from {{ill|Kanroji|ja|甘露寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, {{ill|1942|ja|1942nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * On June 19, {{ill|1946|ja|1946年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, {{ill|1961|ja|1961st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in {{ill|1791|ja|1791st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in {{ill|1798|ja|1798th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited {{ill|Ezochi|ja|蝦夷地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and observed that the {{ill|Ainu|ja|アイヌ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; and that in either {{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of Kansei) or {{ill|1802|ja|1802nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|ja|享和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] [[Category:Kondō Shigekurō]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] [[Category:Kondō Shigekurō]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

{{Shinto Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:Yoshitsune Shrine, Biratori, 2024.jpg|200px|{{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] |所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Honchō 119-1 |緯度度 = 42 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 32.3 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 142 |経度分 = 8 |経度秒 = 10.4 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |例祭 = |本殿 = }} '''Yoshitsune Shrine''' ({{ill|義経神社|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at Honchō 119-1. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Until {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was formally known as "Yoshitsune-sha." A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * In {{ill|1876|ja|1876年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira Mountain|ja|ハヨピラ山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book |title=日高国志料 沙流郡之部 [Hidaka Country Records, Sarugu District Section] |author=御子柴五百彦 |year=1892 |page=18}}). * In {{ill|1884|ja|1884th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 17th year of Meiji) in August – {{ill|Prince Komatsu Akihito|ja|小松宮彰仁親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited and granted offerings for worship. * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the {{ill|Sarugu River|ja|沙流川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and landslides. * In {{ill|1901|ja|1901st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * On November 13, {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the {{ill|Crown Prince|ja|皇太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from {{ill|Kanroji|ja|甘露寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, {{ill|1942|ja|1942nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * On June 19, {{ill|1946|ja|1946年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, {{ill|1961|ja|1961st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in {{ill|1791|ja|1791st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in {{ill|1798|ja|1798th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited {{ill|Ezochi|ja|蝦夷地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and observed that the {{ill|Ainu|ja|アイヌ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; and that in either {{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of Kansei) or {{ill|1802|ja|1802nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|ja|享和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] [[Category:Kondō Shigekurō]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] [[Category:Kondō Shigekurō]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Kondō Shigekurō]]
revision.text: {{Shinto Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:Yoshitsune Shrine, Biratori, 2024.jpg|200px|{{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}}]] |所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}} {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarugu District|qq=}} {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratori Town|qq=}} Honchō 119-1 |緯度度 = 42 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 32.3 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 142 |経度分 = 8 |経度秒 = 10.4 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |例祭 = |本殿 = }} '''Yoshitsune Shrine''' ({{ill|義経神社|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/義経神社|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarugu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratori Town|qq=}} at Honchō 119-1. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. Until {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}}), it was formally known as "Yoshitsune-sha." A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Shrine Administration|qq=}}}} * In {{ill|1876|ja|1876年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1876|qq=}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mura-sha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira Mountain|ja|ハヨピラ山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayopira Mountain|qq=}}{{Cite book |title=日高国志料 沙流郡之部 [Hidaka Country Records, Sarugu District Section] |author=御子柴五百彦 |year=1892 |page=18}}). * In {{ill|1884|ja|1884th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1884|qq=}} (the 17th year of Meiji) in August – {{ill|Prince Komatsu Akihito|ja|小松宮彰仁親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Komatsu Akihito|qq=}} visited and granted offerings for worship. * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1888|qq=}} (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the {{ill|Sarugu River|ja|沙流川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarugu River|qq=}} and landslides. * In {{ill|1901|ja|1901st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1901|qq=}} (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsumawna|qq=}}. * On November 13, {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1906|qq=}} (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1911|qq=}} (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the {{ill|Crown Prince|ja|皇太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince|qq=}} visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from {{ill|Kanroji|ja|甘露寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanroji|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, {{ill|1920|ja|1920th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}}) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}} (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regent|qq=}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}}) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, {{ill|1942|ja|1942nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1942|qq=}} (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}}. * On June 19, {{ill|1946|ja|1946年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1946|qq=}} (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, {{ill|1961|ja|1961st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in {{ill|1791|ja|1791st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}}) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in {{ill|1798|ja|1798th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1798|qq=}} (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Shigekurō|qq=}} visited {{ill|Ezochi|ja|蝦夷地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezochi|qq=}}, and observed that the {{ill|Ainu|ja|アイヌ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ainu|qq=}} of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okikurumi|qq=}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}; and that in either {{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1799|qq=}} (the 11th year of Kansei) or {{ill|1802|ja|1802nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1802|qq=}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|ja|享和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa|qq=}}) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}}) – the shrine building was constructed {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regent|qq=}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}}) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, {{ill|1942|ja|1942nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1942|qq=}} (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}}. * On June 19, {{ill|1946|ja|1946年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1946|qq=}} (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, {{ill|1961|ja|1961st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in {{ill|1791|ja|1791st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}}) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in {{ill|1798|ja|1798th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1798|qq=}} (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Shigekurō|qq=}} visited {{ill|Ezochi|ja|蝦夷地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezochi|qq=}}, and observed that the {{ill|Ainu|ja|アイヌ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ainu|qq=}} of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okikurumi|qq=}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}; and that in either {{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1799|qq=}} (the 11th year of Kansei) or {{ill|1802|ja|1802nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1802|qq=}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|ja|享和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa|qq=}}) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

Akihito|ja|小松宮彰仁親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Komatsu Akihito|qq=}} visited and granted offerings for worship. * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1888|qq=}} (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the {{ill|Sarugu River|ja|沙流川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarugu River|qq=}} and landslides. * In {{ill|1901|ja|1901st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1901|qq=}} (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsumawna|qq=}}. * On November 13, {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1906|qq=}} (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, {{ill|1911|ja|1911年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1911|qq=}} (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the {{ill|Crown Prince|ja|皇太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince|qq=}} visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from {{ill|Kanroji|ja|甘露寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanroji|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, {{ill|1920|ja|1920年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1920|qq=}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}}) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, {{ill|1922|ja|1922年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}} (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regent|qq=}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}}) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, {{ill|1942|ja|1942年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1942|qq=}} (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}}. * On June 19, {{ill|1946|ja|1946年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1946|qq=}} (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, {{ill|1961|ja|1961st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1961|qq=}} (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in {{ill|1791|ja|1791st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}}) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in {{ill|1798|ja|1798年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1798|qq=}} (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Shigekurō|qq=}} visited {{ill|Ezochi|ja|蝦夷地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ezochi|qq=}}, and observed that the {{ill|Ainu|ja|アイヌ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ainu|qq=}} of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okikurumi|qq=}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}; and that in either {{ill|1799|ja|1799年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1799|qq=}} (the 11th year of Kansei) or {{ill|1802|ja|1802年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1802|qq=}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|ja|享和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa|qq=}}) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

{{Shinto Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:Yoshitsune Shrine, Biratori, 2024.jpg|200px|{{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}}]] |所在地 = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}} {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarugu District|qq=}} {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratori Town|qq=}} Honchō 119-1 |緯度度 = 42 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 32.3 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 142 |経度分 = 8 |経度秒 = 10.4 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |例祭 = |本殿 = }} '''Yoshitsune Shrine''' ({{ill|義経神社|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/義経神社|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarugu District|ja|沙流郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarugu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiratori Town|ja|平取町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratori Town|qq=}} at Honchō 119-1. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. Until {{ill|1941|ja|1941年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} (the 16th year of {{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}}), it was formally known as "Yoshitsune-sha." A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Shrine Administration|qq=}}}} * In {{ill|1876|ja|1876年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1876|qq=}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mura-sha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. Until [[:en:1941|1941]] (the 16th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was formally known as "Yoshitsune-sha." A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Shrine Administration|qq=}}}} * In [[:en:1876|1876]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mura-sha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira Mountain|ja|ハヨピラ山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayopira Mountain|qq=}}{{Cite book |title=日高国志料 沙流郡之部 [Hidaka Country Records, Sarugu District Section] |author=御子柴五百彦 |year=1892 |page=18}}). * In [[:en:1884|1884]] (the 17th year of Meiji) in August – [[:en:Prince Komatsu Akihito|Prince Komatsu Akihito]] visited and granted offerings for worship. * In [[:en:1888|1888]] (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the [[:en:Saru River|Sarugu River]] and landslides. * In [[:en:1901|1901]] (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsumawna|qq=}}. * On November 13, [[:en:1906|1906]] (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, [[:en:1911|1911]] (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince]] visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from [[:en:甘露寺|Kanroji]] performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, [[:en:1920|1920]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, [[:en:1922|1922]] (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regent|qq=}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, [[:en:1941|1941]] (the 16th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, [[:en:1941|1941]] (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}}. * On June 19, [[:en:1946|1946]] (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, [[:en:1961|1961]] (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in [[:en:1791|1791]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]]) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in [[:en:1798|1798]] (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Shigekurō|qq=}} visited [[:en:Ezo|Ezochi]], and observed that the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]] of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okikurumi|qq=}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}; and that in either [[:en:1799|1799]] (the 11th year of Kansei) or [[:en:1802|1802]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Kyōwa|Kyōwa]]) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]]

constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in [[:en:1791|1791]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]]) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in [[:en:1798|1798]] (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Shigekurō|qq=}} visited [[:en:Ezo|Ezochi]], and observed that the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]] of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okikurumi|qq=}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}; and that in either [[:en:1799|1799]] (the 11th year of Kansei) or [[:en:1802|1802]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Kyōwa|Kyōwa]]) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:平取町の建築物]]

revision.text: {{Shinto Shrine |名称 = {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:Yoshitsune Shrine, Biratori, 2024.jpg|200px|{{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}}]] |所在地 = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Saru District, Hokkaido|Sarugu District]] [[:en:Biratori, Hokkaido|Hiratori Town]] Honchō 119-1 |緯度度 = 42 |緯度分 = 35 |緯度秒 = 32.3 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 142 |経度分 = 8 |経度秒 = 10.4 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |例祭 = |本殿 = }} '''Yoshitsune Shrine''' ({{ill|義経神社|ja|義経神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/義経神社|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Saru District, Hokkaido|Sarugu District]], [[:en:Biratori, Hokkaido|Hiratori Town]] at Honchō 119-1. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源九郎判官義經|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|lt_ja=源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. Until [[:en:1941|1941]] (the 16th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was formally known as "Yoshitsune-sha." A museum dedicated to Yoshitsune is attached within the shrine grounds. == History == * The exact year of establishment is unknown.{{Cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/ |title={{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義經神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Shrine|qq=}} - "Origin" section |access-date=2023-02-06 |publisher={{ill|Hokkaido Shrine Administration|ja|北海道神社庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Shrine Administration|qq=}}}} * In [[:en:1876|1876]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]) in February – it was Ranked as a {{ill|mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mura-sha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system (at that time, it was located on {{ill|Hayopira Mountain|ja|ハヨピラ山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayopira Mountain|qq=}}{{Cite book |title=日高国志料 沙流郡之部 [Hidaka Country Records, Sarugu District Section] |author=御子柴五百彦 |year=1892 |page=18}}). * In [[:en:1884|1884]] (the 17th year of Meiji) in August – [[:en:Prince Komatsu Akihito|Prince Komatsu Akihito]] visited and granted offerings for worship. * In [[:en:1888|1888]] (the 21st year of Meiji) – the shrine was washed away by flooding of the [[:en:Saru River|Sarugu River]] and landslides. * In [[:en:1901|1901]] (the 34th year of Meiji) – due to flooding of the Sarugu River, a temporary shrine building was constructed at {{ill|Honsumawna|ja|ホンスマウンナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsumawna|qq=}}. * On November 13, [[:en:1906|1906]] (the 39th year of Meiji) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}} (by the Hokkaido Prefecture). * On September 11, [[:en:1911|1911]] (the 44th year of Meiji) – when the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince]] visited Hokkaido, a court attendant from [[:en:甘露寺|Kanroji]] performed a proxy worship. * On August 15, [[:en:1920|1920]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]]) – the shrine building was constructed and relocated. * On July 22, [[:en:1922|1922]] (the 11th year of Taishō) – during a visit to Hokkaido by the {{ill|Regent|ja|摂政宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regent|qq=}}, a court attendant from {{ill|Honda|ja|本多|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda|qq=}} performed a proxy worship. * On March 25, [[:en:1941|1941]] (the 16th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]) – it was Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. * On October 10, [[:en:1941|1941]] (Showa 16) – the name was changed from "Yoshitsune-sha" to "Yoshitsune Shrine." * On January 23, [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of Showa) – it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenheihaku-ryō Kōshin Shrine|qq=}}. * On June 19, [[:en:1946|1946]] (the 21st year of Showa) – it became a religious corporation (Hokkaido No.232). * On August 15, [[:en:1961|1961]] (the 36th year of Showa) – the current shrine building was constructed. == Origin == [[File:Yoshitune-jinja biratori.jpg|thumb|The shrine head from the Meiji to Taishō eras]] According to one theory, the shrine was first established in [[:en:1791|1791]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]]) by Yamada Bun'emon, the contractor for the Sarugu area. Alternatively, another theory holds that in [[:en:1798|1798]] (the 10th year of Kansei) during a northern survey, the retainer {{ill|Kondō Shigekurō|ja|近藤重蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Shigekurō|qq=}} visited [[:en:Ezo|Ezochi]], and observed that the [[:en:Ainu people|Ainu]] of Hiratori revered the hero {{ill|Okikurumi|ja|オキクルミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okikurumi|qq=}} as identical to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}; and that in either [[:en:1799|1799]] (the 11th year of Kansei) or [[:en:1802|1802]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Kyōwa|Kyōwa]]) a small shrine was built on Hayopira Mountain and a wooden statue of Minamoto no Yoshitsune was sculpted by a Buddhist sculptor and enshrined. The inscription on the back of the statue reads: "Kansei 11, Koyoshi, April 28th. Kondō Shigekurō, Fujiwara no Morishige, Hiki Ichirō, Fujiwara no Kanmitsu" with an inscription at its base stating "Edo, Kanda, Sumida Daibutsu Kōhō, Hashi Yoshinori" in ink.Yamagishi Moriyuki, "Yoshitsune Shrine History" (from the introduction of Takase Toshinori's Minamoto no Yoshitsune, the 9th Great Officer, Bumbudō, 1913, pp. 19–20).{{Cite book |author=Ogasawara Shōzō |year=1951 |title=Hokkaidō Takushoku to Jinja [Hokkaido Colonization and Shrines] |publisher=Overseas Emigration Cultural Research Institute |pages=30–33}} It is said that although the shrine was washed away twice by floods, each time the Yoshitsune statue was safely recovered and, under the direction of the village head {{ill|Hiramura Penriuk|ja|平村ペンリウク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramura Penriuk|qq=}}, relocated to its present site.{{Cite book |author=Kihara Naohiko et al. |year=1993 |title=Hokkaido Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten [Hokkaido Historical Figures Encyclopedia] |publisher=Hokkaido Shimbunsha |pages=311}} == Events == * Reiwa 7 Yoshitsune Shrine Annual (Regular) Festival Schedule: * January 1 – 10:00 AM: Saitan Festival * January 15 – 10:00 AM: Dondo Festival * February 2 – Evening: Setsubun Festival * February 6 – 11:00 AM: Hatsuuma Festival (Grand Festival) * February 11 – 10:00 AM: Kigen Festival * February 23 – 10:00 AM: Tenchō Festival * February 28 – 10:00 AM: Yoshitsune Public Birthday Festival (formerly February 1) * March 20 – 11:00 AM: Kin Nen Festival (Grand Festival) * June 30 – Evening: Summer-Crossing Great Purification * August 14 – Evening: Yoimiya Festival * August 15 – 9:00 AM: Regular Grand Festival * Second Sunday of October – Afternoon: Sukēisha Grand Festival (Grand Festival) * November 23 – 11:00 AM: Niinamesai (Grand Festival) * December 31 (New Year’s Eve) – Evening: Year-Crossing Great Purification and Joya Festival == Yoshitsune Museum == * Opening hours: 9:00–17:00; Closed on Mondays. * Admission: Adults ¥200; Children and middle school students ¥100. * Exhibits materials related to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義經|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}. * Also exhibits historical materials related to figures from the Hiratori area. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Yoshitsune Legend|ja|源義経#伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitsune Legend|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yoshitsune-jinja.com/website/ {{ill|Yoshitsune Shrine|ja|義経神社}]] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/義經神社/] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|義経神社|version=103456420|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Hidaka Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Hiratori Town]] [[Category:History of Hiratori Town]] }} [[sn:Yoshitsune Shrine]] [[ja:義経神社]] [[sn:oshitsune Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:平取町の建築物]] [[Category:平取町の歴史]]
